Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Densification, microstructure, and mechanical properties of heat-treated MAR-M247 fabricated by Binder Jetting

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized MAR-M247 powder (D50=15 um) printed on DMP2500 system (Digital Metal AB, Sweden); layer thickness 42 um; aqueous binder C20; saturation Dark 3 (69% pixels saturated); ambient atmosphere at room temperature. Cured at 200C for 8 h; thermal debound at 345C for 3 h; sintered in H800 SF vacuum furnace at 1320C, ~0.1 Pa, 6 h; furnace cooled (<20C/min).
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting (DMP2500, 42 um layer, C20 binder) + sintering (1320C, 6 h, vacuum)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB, Sweden); H800 SF vacuum furnace (Vacua Therm Ltd., UK)

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed gamma-matrix grains with average size 11.3 um; grain boundaries decorated with gamma-prime precipitates and finer precipitate distribution; agglomerations of dark spots (carbides or porosities) mainly along grain boundaries; coarse gamma-prime fraction 1-2 um; finer gamma-prime fraction <1 um; pores irregularly shaped ranging from ~1 um to 20 um in diameter; porosity 2.1-3.3%
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed gamma-matrix (11.3 um avg); gamma-prime precipitates (coarse 1-2 um, fine <1 um) decorating grain boundaries; MC carbides and residual porosity (2.1-3.3%) along grain boundaries

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized MAR-M247 powder (D50=15 um) printed on DMP2500 system (Digital Metal AB, Sweden); layer thickness 42 um; aqueous binder C20; saturation Dark 3 (69% pixels saturated); ambient atmosphere at room temperature. Cured at 200C for 8 h; thermal debound at 345C for 3 h; sintered in H800 SF vacuum furnace at 1320C, ~0.1 Pa, 6 h; furnace cooled (<20C/min). HIP1: 1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h, argon atmosphere, approx. 180C/min cooling rate.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP1 (1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB, Sweden); H800 SF vacuum furnace (Vacua Therm Ltd., UK); Quintus Technologies AB HIP system

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed gamma-matrix grains with average size 10.3 um (no significant grain growth from HIP1); gamma-prime precipitates appear less dense but generally larger compared to as-sintered condition; coarse gamma-prime fraction grown above 5 um at expense of finer fraction; MC carbides (HfC, TaC, TiC, WC) along grain boundaries; remaining porosity visibly reduced; pore and carbide differentiation via backscatter imaging (carbides white, pores black); grain boundary pinning by carbides prevents grain growth
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed gamma-matrix (10.3 um); coarsened gamma-prime precipitates (>5 um coarse fraction); MC carbides decorating grain boundaries; near-full density after HIP1

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized MAR-M247 powder printed on DMP2500 system; layer thickness 42 um; aqueous binder C20; saturation Dark 3. Cured at 200C for 8 h; thermal debound at 345C for 3 h; sintered at 1320C, ~0.1 Pa, 6 h; furnace cooled. HIP2: 1500 bar, 1180C, 4 h, argon atmosphere, approx. 180C/min cooling rate.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP2 (1500 bar, 1180C, 4 h)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB, Sweden); H800 SF vacuum furnace; Quintus Technologies AB HIP system

Microstructure_Text.original: HIP2 temperature 1180C is just below gamma-prime solvus; reduced mass fraction of precipitated gamma-prime; density of dispersed gamma-prime within gamma-grains seems reduced further; coarse gamma-prime fraction has not grown extensively while finer fraction shows even smaller precipitates (<1 um); gamma-prime dissolution and Ostwald ripening occurring simultaneously; MC carbides present along grain boundaries
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Reduced gamma-prime fraction due to near-solvus HIP2 temperature; fine (<1 um) and coarse gamma-prime precipitates; MC carbides along grain boundaries

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 cuboid specimens (DMP2500, 42 um layer); cured, debound, sintered (1320C, 6 h, vacuum); HIP1 (1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h, Ar); HT1: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h followed by age hardening at 700C for 24 h. Heat treatment in Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer (Netzsch GmbH, Germany) in argon atmosphere (99.999% purity).
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP1 + HT1 (solution 1250C + aging 700C/24h)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB); Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer (Netzsch GmbH, Germany)

Microstructure_Text.original: Solution treatment at 1250C dissolved most gamma-prime precipitates (some larger precipitates occasionally still present indicating sluggish dissolution requiring more time); subsequent age hardening at 700C resulted in finest dispersion of gamma-prime precipitates; MC carbides along grain boundaries; etched LOM micrographs show microstructural evolution after HT1
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine dispersion of gamma-prime after solution treatment (1250C) and aging (700C/24h); solution treatment dissolved most gamma-prime but some undissolved coarse precipitates remained

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 cuboid specimens; cured, debound, sintered; HIP1 (1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h); HT2: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h, double-aging (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). Heat treatment in Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer in argon atmosphere.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP1 + HT2 (solution 1250C + double-aging 1000C/12h + 700C/24h) for bimodal gamma-prime distribution

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500; Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer

Microstructure_Text.original: Solution treatment at 1250C dissolved most gamma-prime; double-aging (1000C then 700C) resulted in bimodal gamma-prime size distribution similar to HT1 but with fraction of larger gamma-prime precipitates; coarsest gamma-prime precipitates no longer present leading to genuinely bimodal gamma-prime distribution; MC carbides along grain boundaries
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution achieved through solution treatment and double-aging; finest dispersion with some larger precipitates; favorable microstructure for high-temperature properties

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 cuboid specimens; cured, debound, sintered; HIP1 (1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h); HT3: age hardening only at 700C for 24 h (no solution treatment). Heat treatment in Jupiter STA 449 F1 in argon.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP1 + HT3 (aging only at 700C/24h without solution treatment)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500; Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer

Microstructure_Text.original: Age hardening at 700C without prior solution treatment only leads to coarsening of undissolved gamma-prime precipitates from HIP1 condition; coarse gamma-prime precipitates still present; size distribution of gamma-prime not influenced; high volume fraction of gamma-prime remained from slow furnace cooling after sintering and did not noticeably increase upon further aging; less uniform microstructure compared to HT1 and HT2
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Coarsening of pre-existing gamma-prime precipitates; no new precipitate refinement; less uniform microstructure without solution treatment step

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 cuboid specimens; cured, debound, sintered; HIP1 (1000 bar, 1120C, 4 h); HT4: double-aging without solution treatment (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). Heat treatment in Jupiter STA 449 F1 in argon.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting + sintering + HIP1 + HT4 (double-aging 1000C/12h + 700C/24h without solution treatment)

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500; Jupiter STA 449 F1 thermal analyzer

Microstructure_Text.original: Double-aging at 1000C then 700C without solution treatment resulted in uniformly distributed bimodal gamma-prime distribution compared to as-sintered condition; two additional size ranges of gamma-prime precipitates (~1 um and submicron) formed in addition to pre-existing coarse precipitates; less uniform microstructure compared to HT1 and HT2 with solution treatment
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution achieved without solution treatment; additional size ranges (~1 um and submicron) formed alongside pre-existing coarse precipitates

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 hexagonal tensile specimens (DMP2500, 42 um layer, vertical build orientation); cured at 200C for 8 h; thermal debound at 345C for 3 h; sintered at 1320C, ~0.1 Pa, 6 h; HIP2 (1500 bar, 1180C, 4 h, Ar); HT2: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h then double-aging (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). Tensile bars machined to specification per Fig. 5. Heat treatment of tensile bars in N 7/H furnace (Nabertherm GmbH, Germany) in ambient atmosphere.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting (vertical) + sintering + HIP2 (1500 bar, 1180C) + HT2 (solution 1250C + double-aging) for tensile specimens

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB); H800 SF vacuum furnace (Vacua Therm Ltd.); Quintus Technologies AB HIP system; N 7/H furnace (Nabertherm GmbH, Germany)

Microstructure_Text.original: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution after HT2; fine MC carbides dispersed along grain boundaries; dense microstructure after HIP2 and solution treatment; tensile specimens machined to specification per Fig. 5; horizontal specimens showed no significant anisotropy compared to vertical specimens
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution; dense microstructure; fine carbide distribution along grain boundaries

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 hexagonal tensile specimens (DMP2500, 42 um layer, horizontal build orientation); cured at 200C for 8 h; thermal debound at 345C for 3 h; sintered at 1320C, ~0.1 Pa, 6 h; HIP2 (1500 bar, 1180C, 4 h, Ar); HT2: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h then double-aging (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). Tensile bars machined to specification per Fig. 5. Heat treatment in N 7/H furnace in ambient atmosphere.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting (horizontal) + sintering + HIP2 (1500 bar, 1180C) + HT2 (solution 1250C + double-aging) for tensile specimens

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB); H800 SF vacuum furnace; Quintus Technologies AB HIP system; N 7/H furnace (Nabertherm GmbH, Germany)

Microstructure_Text.original: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution after HT2; fine MC carbides dispersed along grain boundaries; dense microstructure after HIP2 and solution treatment; horizontal specimens showed no significant anisotropy compared to vertical specimens
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime distribution; dense microstructure; fine carbide distribution along grain boundaries

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 hexagonal tensile specimens (DMP2500, vertical build orientation); cured, debound, sintered (1320C, 6 h); HT2: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h then double-aging (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). No HIP treatment for comparison. Tensile bars machined to specification per Fig. 5.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting (vertical) + sintering + HT2 (solution + double-aging) without HIP for comparison

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB); H800 SF vacuum furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: Tensile specimens sintered and heat treated without HIP; HT2 provides bimodal gamma-prime distribution; residual porosity present; comparison baseline without HIP densification
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime after HT2; residual porosity without HIP; comparison group without HIP treatment

Composition_Info.Note: Powder composition determined by ICP-MS; balance Ni computed as 100-sum(other elements)=59.12 wt%

Process_Text.original: Binder Jetting printed MAR-M247 hexagonal tensile specimens (DMP2500, horizontal build orientation); cured, debound, sintered (1320C, 6 h); HT2: solution treatment 1250C for 4 h then double-aging (1000C for 12 h then 700C for 24 h). No HIP treatment for comparison. Tensile bars machined to specification per Fig. 5.
Process_Text.simplified: Binder Jetting (horizontal) + sintering + HT2 (solution + double-aging) without HIP for comparison

Process_Info.Equipment: DMP2500 (Digital Metal AB); H800 SF vacuum furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: Tensile specimens sintered and heat treated without HIP; HT2 provides bimodal gamma-prime distribution; residual porosity present; comparison baseline without HIP densification
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Bimodal gamma-prime after HT2; residual porosity without HIP; comparison group without HIP treatment

Composition_Info.Note: Metal Injection Molding (MIM) MAR-M247 reference from literature [30]; composition comparable to BJ MAR-M247

Process_Text.original: Metal Injection Molding (MIM) MAR-M247 manufactured via debinding and sintering; HIP at 1185C and approx. 170 MPa for 4 h; heat treatment: 1230C for 2 h, 1280C for 2 h, 1080C for 4 h, and 870C for 20 h (multi-step solution and aging treatment) [30].
Process_Text.simplified: MIM MAR-M247: HIP (1185C, 170 MPa, 4 h) + multi-step heat treatment (1230C-1280C-1080C solution + 870C aging)

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed grains with sizes comparable to powder particle size (15-20 um); gamma-prime precipitates in size ranges 0.5-1 um; finely distributed carbide structures (MC, M23C6, M6C) along grain boundaries; remaining porosity approximately 2%; similar to BJ process due to debinding and sintering step where grain boundaries form along powder particle surfaces [30]
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed grains (15-20 um); gamma-prime 0.5-1 um; carbide precipitates along grain boundaries; ~2% residual porosity

Composition_Info.Note: Vacuum Investment Casting (VIC) MAR-M247 reference from literature [45]; composition comparable to BJ MAR-M247

Process_Text.original: Vacuum Investment Casting (VIC) MAR-M247; HIP at 1185C and approx. 170 MPa for 4 h; heat treatment: 1185C for 2 h and 871C for 20 h [45].
Process_Text.simplified: VIC MAR-M247: HIP (1185C, 170 MPa, 4 h) + heat treatment (1185C/2h solution + 871C/20h aging)

Microstructure_Text.original: Dendritic structure with relatively large grains (>200 um) and compositional inhomogeneities on micro- and macroscale; thick gamma-prime plates 5 um; sub-micron gamma-prime precipitates in eutectic pools; large script-like MC carbides with high aspect ratios up to 40 um inside grains; M23C6 carbides at grain boundaries; remaining porosity ~0.25%; solution treatment yields uniform gamma-prime size distribution but slight dendritic segregation remains due to slow diffusion of heavy elements; script-like MC carbides remain inside grains after heat treatment [11,39,45]
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Dendritic structure; large grains (>200 um); thick gamma-prime plates (5 um) and sub-micron precipitates; script-like MC carbides (40 um); 0.25% porosity
